channel.title: Comments for page "Skeeve Online"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Skeeve Online"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone know where I can find more 2662 content, if there is any?

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
All images were modified from the following:

Basket Star by Ed Bierman: https://www.flickr.com/photos/edbierman/3560675735
Pacific Sea Peach by Ed Bierman: https://www.flickr.com/photos/edbierman/3556517155
Christmas Week Diving by Ed Bierman: https://www.flickr.com/photos/edbierman/25527306818

item.title: Re: Unexpected tearjerker, what the heck.
item.content:encoded:
On the third tentacle

This is a great phrase and totally thematically appropriate.

I admit that I didn't even know about this until I saw the above comment. When I saw both 2662 and Beauremont get mentioned in the conversation, I was curious as to what sort of story would be happening… and then when I saw the format setup, I was intrigued.

Take my upvote (to get this to +69, incidentally)!

item.title: Re: Unexpected tearjerker, what the heck.
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

A bit of both and more.

On the one hand, it's a story about Thul's continued frustration with just being itself without the constant (anomalous) societal pressure to be something it's not interested in. In this reading, Beauremont is affected by Thul's anomaly and turns to worship it.

On the other hand, it's a story about rampant dick pics. And Beauremont is ultimately a purveyor of cowardly but harmless offensive garbage.

On the third tentacle, Thul's life is Lovecraftian and beyond the ken of mere mortals, and so the sort of happenstance where it just meets someone who would lure someone in just to spam them with dick pics is far more preordained and far more random than you or I could ever dare to comprehend.

All in all, yes, Thul's anomaly turned Beauremont into a worshipper, but Beauremont is the sort of being who would send unsolicited inappropriate images in the first place. So, what does Beauremont do when suddenly face-to-face with the god-being that turns it on? …Yeah.

item.title: Re: Unexpected tearjerker, what the heck.
item.content:encoded:
Also, I'd love to have Word of God on whether Beauremont was

+ spoilers
- spoilers

inadvertently brainwashed by… whatever it is poor Thul has that causes him to get cults, or if he was intending this from the beginning.

item.title: Unexpected tearjerker, what the heck.
item.content:encoded:
I, uh, might have teared up a little reading this.

I was so happy when LB was understanding and supportive of Thul… "You really think I'm not weird or broken?" struck me too close to home. I know how it feels, poor guy.

And my kinky self was really happy with the convo that begins with "I just want… you."

AND THEN THE REVEAL. THE WHIPLASH HIT ME LIKE A SLAP IN THE FACE.

WHY AM I CRYING.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ spoiler
That was the idea. You win a cookie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the Necropost, but I was wondering that too as I read so I'm postulating this for posterity: could it be that skip with the really long arm that claims to be stuck in a stove?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "26Thulu62 is VERY GOD!" I know the TotleighTalk isn't everybody's cup of tea, but you had some good ones in this :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well WrongJohn, you were right about this being kind of uncomfortable, but it was a good and well written story, even the uncomfortable parts were funny, especially the Amnestic comment.
Massy judj storee uproot!
Edit: Although something in my gut told me that Beauremont was up to no good. And I still wonder who Wilf is or how they got stuck in the washing machine…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, that escalated quickly.

And ouch, poor 2662, who just wants to be an Eldritch Abomination without a messed up cult to deal with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do I not feel bad for the world SCP-1322 is connected to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I decided I hadn't improved the connection between the game and the game world enough. I tweaked the story slightly. Should fill it out a bit better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I heard the word "deathfucking" I was horrified. You crossed that line so big.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no words. This almost feels like a modern cool war. Enough batshit crazy crackness to make your brain spin, but enough serious stuff and characterization to make it relevant and important beyond the initial tale.

I need to think about this one…but I think I might upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is how you cross the skips. :D I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WELCORM toward TotleighSoft Custormer Supports! What helpcan? FREQUENT ASK QUESTROOMS? REPORT USAR? > Report Usar OH GEES! This is serious issue, yes. Consult [data not found] > Report User YES! Very. Anything else? > Report User LordBeauremont [data not found]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt so sorry for that unfortunate eldritch horror. It just can't catch a break.

Having read some of the accounts on CreepyPMs (a Reddit forum for creeped upon), the ending was particularly poignant. It thought it had found a friend, and Beurement had to go and do… that.

item.title: +1 +1 +1 Ske/eve/ Online you clever shit
item.content:encoded: I'm very biased in favor of articles that allow people to exist in their nonhumanity and strangeness and interact in both pleasant and terrible ways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2662 moves to the shower stall and turns on the water. It sits in the shower stall for the remainder of the incident.

Poor emo-dritch entity just can't catch a break.

So many great lines in the dialogue here, and I always love tales that show a slice of life in the SCP-verse like this, just letting the fantastic and the mundane mingle on a human humanoid individual level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just speechless about this… it's bizarre, and I'm not sure what to make of it for now…

Edit: It is somewhat entertaining, but the premises is rather out of the blue and feels forcefully implanted there to begin the whole story.

While I appreciate what it does for 2662, I do not like how 1171 is given the characterisation of an online harasser/troll (which is frankly out of the blue).

Unless 1171 is actually a troll (the mythical ones)…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "Massy's Big Chance" style "misspelled for humor" sections aren't very entertaining, because they're just… misspelled. There's an inkling of a cool story with the Glory Hole crossover, but that never amounts to anything.

The climax with the images made me feel uncomfortable. Getting harassed sexually over the internet, especially by a person who has gradually earned your trust is… well, that just makes me feel anxious.

Verdict: Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haven't read the articles, but I quite enjoy this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The best worst part about this is that it's such a dead ringer for a multitude of gaming online conversations I've seen or experienced. The most unrealistic part of this is that they're both using perfect grammar and spelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some of you may have heard of my attempt to crosstest SCP-2662 with SCP-1171. I ended up also crosstesting SCP-2527 and SCP-1322 in the process.

…With a few Easter Eggs.

Because who knows who you end up meeting online?

I would like to thank JackIke and psul for review, and especially SoullessSingularity for support throughout and Cyantreuse for dedication and help with the images.
